title: Gin Is Torn Between Krieg and Sanji
description: On pages 10–13, the fight against Krieg reaches a moral breaking point as Gin, Sanji, Zeff, and Luffy clash over debt, survival, and strength.
scene: Krieg’s weapons and cruelty force everyone at the Baratie to reveal what they truly value: victory, loyalty, survival, or protecting someone else’s dream.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Gin! Just blow that damn geezer's head off!

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Hey, I haven't laid a single finger on you guys at all.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: All I did was break the fins.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: The hell do you think you're doing, errand boy!?

dialogue.speaker: Gin
dialogue.text: Don't just stand there! Kill Zeff already!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Are you out of your mind!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Just what the hell for do you think I've been working in this restaurant!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: But if the ship's destroyed, their plans will be ruined.

dialogue.speaker: Gin
dialogue.text: You have no idea what this restaurant means to me,

dialogue.speaker: Gin
dialogue.text: Nor the gravity of my debt!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: So you're planning to die for this restaurant?

summary: This part pushes the Baratie battle toward its climax by contrasting Krieg’s ruthless power with Luffy and Sanji’s stubborn ideals.
